areasReviewed: WASM imports, exports, and bounded memory
Hidden data channels and ambient authority

findings.evidence: Cargo.lock pins registry packages by checksum and the Tine SDK to Git revision d73e866b44bff140c6f376d4a7659627c3484dad; the matching locked SDK source is included. No build scripts are declared.
findings.impact: Reproducibility and reviewability are good, though rebuilding still requires trusting the pinned registry and Git artifacts to match the reviewed sources.
findings.title: Pinned build dependencies retain ordinary supply-chain exposure

findings.evidence: tine_alloc creates a Vec with caller-supplied capacity, while MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES is checked later in tine_handle. The WASM imports only host-provided memory capped by the linker configuration at 16 MiB.
findings.impact: A hostile host could request an oversized allocation and cause a trap or invocation failure, but the plugin cannot trigger this itself and gains no ambient authority or persistence from it.
findings.title: SDK allocation limit is enforced after allocation

aiReview.summary: The plugin is a minimal declarative block-decoration contribution whose handler always returns an empty effect list. The manifest, source, locked SDK, deterministic WASM report, and README are consistent. No malicious graph transformation, protocol abuse, hidden channel, ambient-authority access, or meaningful denial-of-service behavior is evident.
